item.title: Beta 1.1.4 (build 71)
item.description:
Beta build 71

  • Adds connection outcome toasts after connect operations.
  • Single-device success toasts include the connected device name.
  • Batch success toasts summarize the number of connected devices instead of listing names.
  • Includes related state-handling and interaction-lock test coverage.

What to watch: verify single-connect naming, 2+ device batch count messaging, clean truncation for long device names, and no stale/stacked toasts across repeated connect attempts.

item.title: Version 1.1.4 (build 70)
item.description:
What's New in 1.1.4 (build 70)

  • LaunchGuard: automatically repairs and relaunches the app if terminated due to menu bar host instability.
  • Licensing support with LemonSqueezy integration.
  • New License settings pane.
  • Onboarding refinements.
  • Bug fixes and stability improvements.

item.title: Beta 1.1.4 (build 69)
item.description:
Beta build 69

  • License pane and onboarding panel refinements.
  • LaunchGuard: auto-repair and relaunch on menu bar host termination.
  • v1 licensing implementation.

item.title: Beta 1.1.4 (build 68)
item.description:
Beta build 68

  • LaunchGuard: automatically repairs and relaunches the app if terminated prematurely due to menu bar host instability.
  • v1 licensing implementation.

item.title: Version 1.1.4 (build 67)
item.description:
What's New in 1.1.4

  • Switching several devices at once is now more reliable and stable.
  • A brand-new onboarding flow to help you get set up.
  • Better overall stability.
  • Bug fixes and faster single-device switching.

item.title: Beta 1.1.4 (build 66)
item.description:
Beta build 66 — onboarding copy refinement

Dev-facing notes — this build only reaches Switchy Beta (channel beta).

  • Refined onboarding step 3 copy: now points users to "Connect All" for switching every device at once, and explains the closed-lid MacBook power tip more clearly (plug in + enable keep-running in Preferences, with the battery trade-off called out).

What to watch: the third onboarding "how to use" screen — verify the new copy reads well and the "Connect All" guidance matches the actual menu-bar action.

item.title: Beta 1.1.4 (build 65)
item.description:
Beta build 65 — onboarding polish + notification icon

Dev-facing notes — this build only reaches Switchy Beta (channel beta).

  • Visual improvements to onboarding (layout, spacing, copy).
  • Fixed notification icon still using the old asset.
  • Onboarding crop tweaked for the device-illustration frame.
  • Removed the "beta" tag label shown during multi-device connection — Switchy Beta no longer advertises itself as beta in-app.
  • Docs only: corrected a stale power-off comment in handlePeerAcquireRequest (lever E). No behavior change.
  • Test pin: 3-device batch progress series asserted non-decreasing. Regression guard for the lever A–E work from build 63.

What to watch: onboarding screens on first launch / re-onboarding, and that the multi-device connect flow no longer shows a "beta" label anywhere. Batch-connect progress still tracks per-device state correctly through a 3-device batch.

item.title: Beta 1.1.4 (build 64)
item.description:
Beta build 64 — progress bar UI polish

Dev-facing notes — this build only reaches Switchy Beta (channel beta).

  • Improved batch-connect progress bar UI.
  • No functional changes to the batch-connect reliability levers (A–E) shipped in build 63 — this is a UI-only follow-up. Watch that progress reporting still tracks per-device state correctly through a multi-device batch.

item.title: Beta 1.1.4 (build 63)
item.description:
Beta build 63 — batch reliability overhaul (Levers A–E) + review findings

Dev-facing notes — this build only reaches Switchy Beta (channel beta).

Lever A: releaseAgeSeconds backdating (PROTOCOL CHANGE, biggest win)

  • The 5s pair floor was measured from the peer's reply, but the peer unpaired the device ~5s before replying (0.5+0.5s waits + 4s auth window). Pairing actually started ~10s after the device was freed — 2× the proven envelope.
  • Protocol change: peer now reports releaseAgeSeconds (time since unpair completed) in the acquire response. Acquirer backdates releaseConfirmedAt by the age, capped at the 4s auth window (maxReleaseAgeCredit) so pairing still starts ≥1s past window end. Old peers → nil → 0 credit → current behavior (backward-compatible).
  • Expected: D0 floor waits ~1s (was 5s); D1 gate ~0.8–1.1s (was ~4.8s).

Lever B: conditional inter-device settle (0.5s success / 3s failure)

  • Since the collateral BT power-off skip (build 62), a mid-batch release with the next device still peer-connected never powers the radio off — the radio is already up and the next device is connected. After a SUCCESS, 0.5s is pacing only (was 3s). After a FAILURE, keeps 3s (pairPhaseComplete → peer power-on ~1–3s must land before the next release request).
  • Also bumps the peer-side radio-on guard sleep 500ms → 2s (controller init takes ~2s before accepting commands reliably).

Lever C: remove pre-flight power cycle, replace with radio-on guard

  • The §13 rationale (clear stale HCI link keys) was disproven by §14's HCI capture (0x02 is device-side). acquireInternal already unpairs locally per device before re-pairing. The power cycle cost ~3.4s and dropped local-fast-path orphans mid-batch. Replaced with a cheap probe + conditional powerOnBluetooth (only if the radio is actually off).

Lever D: verifyConnection 3→10 attempts (early-exit on first positive)

  • July 2026 field run showed a healthy D1 pair+connect succeed but the verification probe take ~2s — exceeding the 3×300ms=0.9s budget and routing the device into ~9s of unnecessary recovery. Layer-1 (verifyConnection) is a bounded fast-fail for genuinely failed --connect; layer-2 (ConnectionQualityVerifier, 5s budget) is the authoritative settle gate. Layer-1 must not throw on merely-slow verification. 10×300ms=3s budget; early-exits on first positive so happy path stays ~0.3–0.6s.

Lever E: remove release-side BT power-off entirely (always-on radio)

  • The power-off's stated purpose (prevents device auto-reconnect) is already fulfilled by unpair-while-connected + the 4s auth-rejection window: the device's pairing record is gone, every reconnect is auth-rejected, and by window end its firmware has cleared keys and is advertising. Build 62's field run proved it (BT stayed on, device auth-rejected, became pairable, peer acquired it).
  • Always keeping BT on eliminates the collateral-drop wedge class at the root — including non-tracked BT devices (AirPods, etc.) that build 62's collateral check didn't cover. Sequential switches stop paying power-off → power-on churn (~3.5s). Recovery finds the peer radio already up.
  • Dormant power-on machinery (scheduleBluetoothPowerOn, triggerEarlyBluetoothPowerOn, reconnectCollateralDevices, radio-on guard, fallback constant + invariant test) kept for now — cleanup in a follow-up after field verify.

Review findings 1–4 (robustness/coverage/doc-debt)

  • Set previousDeviceSucceeded=false on the release-failed continue path (was stale — flag name now matches behavior in all paths).
  • Added NetworkMessage codec round-trip test for releaseAgeSeconds — pins the silent-drop trap (encode/decode with the field + legacy-blob-without-key → nil).
  • Updated stale doc-comments on handlePeerAcquireRequest (no longer powers off BT, no longer uses 45s fallback, returns PeerAcquireOutcome); two other trivial stale comments.

Look at

  • Batch acquire latency. D0 should feel dramatically faster (~1s floor vs 5s); D1 gate ~0.8–1.1s vs ~4.8s. End-to-end batch of 2 should be noticeably snappier.
  • Sequential switches. No power-off/power-on churn between devices. Recovery should find peer radio already up.
  • Non-tracked BT devices (AirPods, etc.): should no longer be dropped as collateral during a release. Verify your AirPods stay connected through a switch.
  • Slow verification path. A device that takes ~2s to verify should no longer fall into ~9s of recovery — it should settle in the verifier's 5s budget.
  • Protocol compat. If you mix this beta with an older peer, the old peer reports no releaseAgeSeconds → 0 credit → old 5s floor. Upgrade both Macs to see the full win.

Known risks

  • Lever E is only field-verified for the non-last device (D0). The last device is theoretically identical but untested. Watch for 0x02/719 on the last device and on sequential switches.
  • Lever A protocol change — if a peer reports a bogus releaseAgeSeconds (negative, huge, or wrong), the cap at maxReleaseAgeCredit (4s) bounds the damage, but a too-small age could start pairing before the device is actually pairable. The cap is conservative (4s = full auth window).
  • Lever C radio-on guard adds a probe to every acquire. If the probe misreads the radio state it could power on when unnecessary (harmless) or fail to power on when needed (wedge). Watch for "radio off" wedges on cold starts.
  • Dormant power-on machinery is still in the code — not called in the normal path but present. Cleanup pending field verification.

item.title: Beta 1.1.4 (build 62)
item.description:
Beta build 62 — field fix: skip BT power-off when collateral devices are still connected

Dev-facing notes — this build only reaches Switchy Beta (channel beta).

Root cause of the 100% second-device failure

  • The per-device JIT release powered off BT after D0's release, which dropped D1 (the mouse) as a collateral device. When BT came back on, D1 couldn't reconnect because its keychain link key was missing (result 150). The next release request found D1 disconnected, the unpair went out LOCAL-ONLY (firmware never heard it), D1 kept stale keys → 0x02/719 wedge on the peer's pair attempt.

The fix (Phase-9 §15 field fix)

  • After the auth-rejection window, the release now checks whether other tracked devices are still connected. If yes, it keeps BT ON and skips the power-off/power-on cycle.
  • Safe because: the released device's pairing records are gone (it can't reconnect to this Mac — auth-rejected), and its firmware already cleared keys during the 4s window. It becomes pairable with this Mac's radio up. The next device's release then finds it CONNECTED and the unpair signal reaches its firmware.
  • Same collateral check applied to the error fallback path for consistency.
  • Side benefit: non-last releases skip the ~1.3s power-off + ~3s power-on wait, addressing the latency regression reported for the 1st device.

Look at

  • Batch release of 2+ devices from one Mac. This is the exact scenario that was 100% failing on the 2nd device. Should now release cleanly end-to-end.
  • Single-device release: behavior should be unchanged (no collateral → BT still powers off, then scheduled power-on).
  • Latency: the 1st device in a batch should feel faster (no power-off/power-on cycle until the last device).

Known risks

  • If BT stays on for a non-last release, the released device is pairable and our radio is up. The peer must acquire it before any other device event can grab it. In practice the peer drives the acquire immediately after the release response, so this should be fine — but watch for racing where a third Mac tries to pair the just-released device.
  • The collateral check uses connectionState.isConnected on tracked devices. If a connected device isn't in trackedDevices it won't count as collateral and BT will still power off. Verify tracked-devices list is complete in your test setup.

item.title: Beta 1.1.4 (build 61)
item.description:
Beta build 61 — batch release reliability hardening

Dev-facing notes — this build only reaches Switchy Beta (channel beta).

Peer-side radio-on guard (Phase-9 §15)

  • handlePeerAcquireRequest now probes the peer's Bluetooth radio and powers it on if it's still down from a previous chained release. This ensures the unpair signal always reaches the device — an unheard unpair leaves stale firmware keys and a durable wedge.
  • Look at: multi-device batch switching where one Mac releases several devices in a chain. Previously the radio could stay off after an earlier release and the next unpair would be silently lost.

interDeviceReleaseSettle (3s)

  • New 3s settle at the start of each per-device loop iteration gives the peer's radio time to come back before the next release request needs it. Replaces the redundant end-of-loop interPairSettle.
  • Look at: timing between consecutive device releases in a batch — should feel no slower overall but more reliable.

Refactor pass

  • Cleanup of AppState.swift and BatchConnectTiming from the review pass (commit 32dab1e). No behavior change intended — watch for regressions in batch acquire/release flows.

Known risks

  • The radio-on guard adds a probe step to every peer acquire request; if it misfires it could power the radio on when we meant to leave it off. Verify single-device switching still powers off cleanly after release.
  • interDeviceReleaseSettle replaces interPairSettle — if anything relied on the old end-of-loop timing it may shift.

item.title: Beta 1.1.4 (build 60)
item.description:
Beta build 60

Dev-facing notes — this build only reaches Switchy Beta (channel beta).

First-run onboarding tour

  • New onboarding tour shown on first launch and reachable from Settings. Walks through how switching works, the connect-all flow, and the "When connected to power, keep Switchy running" option.
  • Look at: slide order, copy, screenshots, and whether the tour re-shows correctly from Settings.

Renamed lid / keep-awake setting

  • "Keep this Mac available for switching" is now "When connected to power, keep Switchy running" with a clearer description of what it does (keeps Switchy running while plugged in, even with the lid closed, so other Macs can switch devices without opening the lid).
  • Look at: the General settings row and the notification / hint copy that mentions the setting by name.

Known risks

  • Onboarding copy and slide order still settling — feedback welcome.
  • Keep-awake behavior is unchanged; only labels and descriptions moved.

item.title: Version 1.1.4 (build 59)
item.description:
What's New in 1.1.4 (build 59)

This update makes switching more reliable, clearer, and easier to use. Here's what changed since the last update:

Multi-Device Connect is now a Beta feature

Connecting several devices at once ("Connect all from [Mac]") is handy, but it wasn't as reliable as switching one device at a time — the second device would sometimes fail to pair because of Bluetooth hardware limitations outside Switchy's control.

To keep things simple and dependable, Multi-Device Connect is now off by default. The reliable path — connecting devices one at a time — is what you'll see out of the box.

If you relied on connecting multiple devices at once, you can turn it back on: open Settings → General and enable Multi-Device Connect (Beta). It's labeled Beta because it can still be hit-and-miss with some Bluetooth controllers.

Smoother switching under the hood

  • Fewer "stuck on the wrong Mac" moments. We fixed a race condition where, during a switch, the device could briefly re-connect to the Mac you're switching away from right as it was supposed to pair with the new one. The two Macs now coordinate so the old one keeps its Bluetooth off until the new Mac is done pairing.
  • Automatic recovery when a second device won't pair. If a device fails to pair during a multi-device switch, Switchy now does a clean Bluetooth reset and tries again on a fresh connection. This recovers most of the failures that used to need a manual retry.

Clearer status and errors

  • Better status messages in the menu after a device disconnects or reconnects, so you always know what's happening.
  • Simpler error panel. When a device can't be reached, the retry button is now right under the message — no extra clutter.
  • Clearer settings copy for the Multi-Device Connect option.

As always, single-device switching — the core of Switchy — stays rock solid. Thanks for using Switchy!

item.title: Beta 1.1.4 (build 58)
item.description:
Beta build 58 — Settings copy update

For developer testing only. Small copy fix in the multi-device settings section. No switching-logic changes since build 57.

What changed

  • Updated the multi-device settings menu copy for clarity.

What to test

  • Open Settings → General and confirm the multi-device copy reads correctly.
  • Single-device and batch switching should behave identically to build 57.

item.title: Beta 1.1.4 (build 57)
item.description:
Beta build 57 — Multi-Device Connect is now opt-in (Beta-gated) + error panel cleanup

For developer testing only. Gates the unreliable batch "Connect all" flow behind an off-by-default Settings toggle and simplifies the unavailable-device panel.

What changed

  • Multi-Device Connect is now opt-in. Added a multiDeviceConnectEnabled preference (OFF by default) in Settings → General, tagged Beta. The "Connect all from [Mac]" menu section and the connectAllFromPeer entry point are now gated behind this toggle. Rationale: batch connect is unreliable — a second device can fail with 0x02 due to Bluetooth/SSP hardware limitations outside Switchy's control. When off, users connect devices one at a time (the reliable path).
  • Unavailable-device panel simplified. Removed the contextual "keep awake" education label that named the peer Mac (shouldShowKeepAwakeEducation, educationLabel). The retry button now always sits directly below the description — no more conditional layout branching.
  • Settings divider fix. The "Device Discovery Alerts" row now renders with a divider for consistent spacing.

What to test

  • Toggle OFF by default: On a fresh install (or after clearing multiDeviceConnectEnabled from UserDefaults), "Connect all from [Mac]" should NOT appear in the menu even when a peer has multiple available devices. Single-device connect should work as before.
  • Toggle ON: Enable "Multi-Device Connect" in Settings → General. The "Connect all from [Mac]" section should reappear in the menu and the batch flow should work as in build 56.
  • Persistence: The toggle state should survive app restarts (stored in UserDefaults via setMultiDeviceConnectEnabled).
  • Unavailable-device panel: Trigger an unavailable-device panel (e.g. disconnect a peer mid-acquire). Confirm there is no "keep awake" education line and the retry button sits directly under the description with clean spacing.
  • Single-device switching unaffected. The core acquire/release flow should be unchanged.

Known risks

  • Existing beta testers lose "Connect all" silently. The toggle ships OFF, so anyone relying on batch connect will see it disappear until they enable it in Settings. This is intentional (the flow was unreliable), but worth noting if anyone reports "Connect all is gone."

item.title: Beta 1.1.4 (build 56)
item.description:
Beta build 56 — Sleeping Devices: accurate UI status for asleep peers

For developer testing only. Fixes the false "Available" status shown for devices on a peer Mac that is asleep with "Wake for network access" enabled (Bonjour Sleep Proxy keeps it visible).

What changed

  • New "Sleeping Devices" section. Devices on a Bonjour-visible-but-unreachable peer now appear under "Sleeping Devices" with a moon icon and "Wake the Mac to connect" guidance — instead of falsely showing as "Available" and then failing with a 60s timeout when clicked.
  • TCP-verified reachability. A new isPeerReachable predicate replaces Bonjour-only checks in the availability logic. A peer is only considered reachable if: (a) it sent fresh device announcements within 5 min, OR (b) an active reverse TCP channel exists, OR (c) it responded to a keep-alive probe within 30s. Bonjour visibility alone is no longer sufficient.
  • confirmedPeers in keep-alive pool. A peer is only "confirmed" after responding to at least one real pong, not just because pinging started. Closes the 30s "assume alive" window that made freshly-rediscovered Sleep Proxy zombies appear reachable.
  • Immediate first probe. Keep-alive's first probe now fires on peer discovery (deadline: .now()) instead of waiting 10s. A zombie is detected within ~2s instead of 10s.
  • Batch connect early-exit. "Connect all from [peer]" aborts immediately with the unavailable panel if the peer is not reachable — no more 2s wait + 60s timeout hang on a sleeping peer.
  • "Connect all" button hidden for sleeping peers. hasLivePeerData now requires isPeerReachable, so the button won't appear for asleep peers.
  • Stale cleanup fixes. cleanupStaleDevices now removes entries from unreachable peers (was: only from Bonjour-offline peers). Sleep Proxy zombies no longer accumulate stale entries.
  • Docs cleaned up. Removed stale WOL/WakeManager claims from Zombie-Peer-Detection.md and Network-Acquire-Speed-Optimizations.md. Marked Wake-on-LAN-Source-of-Truth.md as aspirational/not-implemented.

What to test

  • Sleep Proxy scenario (the reported bug): On Mac A, enable "Wake for network access", connect 2 devices, put Mac A to sleep (close lid on AC power). Wait 2+ min. On Mac B, open Switchy menu — devices should show under "Sleeping Devices" with moon icon, NOT "Available Devices". No "Connect all from Mac A" button should appear.
  • No false "Available" flash: Right after Mac A sleeps, open the menu on Mac B. Within ~2s the devices should move to "Sleeping" (not linger as "Available" for 30s).
  • Zsceler/asymmetric peers unaffected: If you have a firewalled peer (TCP-unreachable but Bonjour-visible with a reverse channel), its devices should still show as "Available" — the activePeerConnections.getReady signal protects this path.
  • Normal switching still works: Devices on an awake, reachable peer should show as "Available" and connect normally. Batch "Connect all" should work as before.
  • Browser restarts unaffected: If you toggle WiFi or the Bonjour browser restarts, devices should not flash through "Sleeping" — the isPeerOnlineForAvailability grace path is preserved.
  • Peer wakes up: After Mac A wakes, its devices should move back to "Available" once fresh announcements arrive or a keep-alive pong succeeds.

Known risks

  • First-discovery window (~2s): A just-discovered peer has no pong and no announcements yet. During this brief window its devices may show as "Sleeping" until the first probe completes. The immediate-first-probe change minimizes this, but if you see a healthy peer flash "Sleeping" on first discovery, report it.
  • No click-to-wake. Sleeping devices are informational only — clicking does nothing. The user must manually wake the peer Mac. WOL/click-to-wake remains a future feature (the extendedTimeout plumbing is already in NetworkManager but not activated).

item.title: Beta 1.1.4 (build 55)
item.description:
Beta build 55 — 0x02/719 recovery: disconnect-based + single-device path

For developer testing only. Reworks the second-device 0x02/719 recovery and extends it to the single-device acquire path.

What changed

  • Root-cause theory updated. The 0x02/719 is now understood as a device-side SSP abort (device withholds user-confirmation after the IO-Capability exchange), INDEPENDENT of the acquiring controller's clean/dirty state. HCI proof: an identical abort on a 0-ACL controller (~/btd-fail 2026-06-21). Replaces the build-54 "clean controller" theory.
  • Disconnect-based recovery replaces power-cycle. Recovery now tears down survivor ACL links via --disconnect (radio stays on → no auto-reconnect wall, no keyboard/input drop), re-pairs the failed device, reconnects survivors via connect-only (bonds survive --disconnect), then ONE delayed isolated retry before the panel. Replaces build 54's power-cycle approach.
  • Single-device path now recovers before the panel. Recovery is extracted into a shared recoverFailedDevices helper used by BOTH batch and single acquire paths. A single-device 0x02/no-connection failure now gets disconnect-based recovery BEFORE the "device unavailable" panel — if recovery succeeds, the panel is skipped entirely.
  • Probe hardening. isPairedOrNil now retries with backoff (2 attempts, 200ms) before returning inconclusive; exposed as internal for unit testing.

What to test

  • Single-device acquire from a peer that fails with 0x02 — should now self-heal instead of showing the panel. This is the new path in build 55.
  • Batch "Connect All" with 2 devices — second-device 0x02 should still self-heal (same behavior as build 54, now via the shared helper).
  • No input drop during recovery — radio stays on throughout (unlike build 54's power cycle). Verify no keyboard/mouse stutter when a sibling is disconnected/reconnected around the re-pair.
  • If a failure occurs, capture both logs: log stream --predicate 'process == "bluetoothd"' and log stream --predicate 'subsystem == "com.switchy"'. Look for [PAIR DIAG] recovery controller=... before sibling disconnect → Recovery re-pair → sibling reconnect → optional delayed isolated retry.

item.title: Beta 1.1.4 (build 54)
item.description:
Beta build 54 — batch second-device pairing: clean-controller recovery

For developer testing only. New root cause + fix for the intermittent second-device 0x02/719 orphan in batch "connect all".

What changed

  • Root cause (fresh capture): a fresh --pair reliably succeeds on a freshly power-cycled clean controller and intermittently fails on a dirty one (a sibling already bonded+connected). The first device is reliable only because the pre-flight power cycle hands it a clean controller; the second never gets that — and long idle waits don't help because the connected sibling keeps the controller dirty.
  • Clean-controller recovery: on a second-device 0x02, Switchy now power-cycles once, re-pairs the failed device first on the clean controller, then reconnects the surviving device(s). Cascade-safe (survivors always reconnected) and bounded to one cycle. Replaces the old 1.5s retry that re-paired on the still-dirty controller.
  • Hardening: reference-counted release guard (fixes a same-device overlapping-release race) and a [PAIR DIAG] controller=clean|dirty log before every fresh pair.

What to test

  • From a second Mac, "Connect All" with 2 devices (Mouse + Trackpad) repeatedly. The second device should now self-heal instead of orphaning — you should NOT see the "device unavailable" panel.
  • If a failure occurs, capture both logs (Phase-9 §13): log stream --predicate 'process == "bluetoothd"' and log stream --predicate 'subsystem == "com.switchy"'. Look for controller=dirty before the failing --pair, then Recovery power cycle → Clean-controller re-pair → survivor reconnect.

item.title: Version 1.1.4 (build 52)
item.description:
What's New in 1.1.4

  • Much more reliable multi-device switching. "Connect All" and back-to-back device switches no longer intermittently fail on the second device.
  • Out-of-band detection. Devices connected through System Settings → Bluetooth are now correctly shown as connected instead of "Unavailable".
  • Magic Trackpad fix. Resolved a case where a Trackpad could disconnect or show as unavailable right after connecting.
  • Additional Bluetooth connection stability improvements.

item.title: Beta 1.1.3 (build 51)
item.description:
Beta build 51 — D1 second-device pairing reliability + diagnostics

For developer testing only. Targets the intermittent second-device (D1) pair failure (0x02 / SSP 719) in batch "connect all" and immediate sequential switching.

What changed

  • Serialized all blueutil execution. Every controller command (pair/connect/unpair/probe/info) now runs through one serial queue, so a stray probe can't land inside another device's SSP handshake — the suspected concurrent-controller-contention race. In the normal serial acquire flow this adds zero delay.
  • Gated the ownership-conflict verifier. Its hardware-probe no longer fires while any acquire/batch is in progress (was un-gated, could fire a blueutil probe mid-pair).
  • Recovery no longer destroys a good bond on a probe error. A failed isPaired probe used to be read as "not paired" and triggered a destructive full re-pair on a still-bonded device (re-introducing 719). It now only re-pairs on a definite negative; any uncertainty preserves the bond.
  • New [BT-SERIAL] diagnostic logging. Each blueutil logs how long it queued behind another and how long it ran + exit status — so a failure capture can prove whether anything overlapped D1's --pair.

What to test

  • From a second Mac, "Connect All" with 2 devices (Mouse + Trackpad) repeatedly — watch whether the second device still fails intermittently.
  • Sequential: switch D0, then immediately switch D1 — same watch.
  • If a failure occurs, capture logs: /usr/bin/log stream --predicate 'subsystem == "com.switchy"' --info and look at the [BT-SERIAL] lines around the failing --pair (queued ~0ms + nothing overlapping = serialization is clean → cause is release-side/device-readiness, not contention).

item.title: Beta 1.1.3 (build 50)
item.description:
Beta build 50 — fix info-connected-without-battery classification

For developer testing only.

What changed

  • Fix: trackpad disconnects immediately after acquire. classifyProbe treated info.isConnected == true with no battery as a ghost (bluetoothConnectedOnly). blueutil doesn't expose battery for Magic Trackpad even when it's working. The settle gate always timed out, then clearStaleLocalPairing unpaired the working device. Fix: info.isConnected == true is now sufficient for usableConnected — battery is optional.
  • Fix: menu still shows "Unavailable" after manual System Settings pairing. Same root cause — the inverse reconcile pass got bluetoothConnectedOnly from the settle gate and refused to reconcile.
  • Updated ConnectionQuality doc comments and added 4 regression tests covering info-connected-without-battery for both acquire and inverse reconcile paths.

What to test

  • Connect Magic Trackpad via System Settings → open Switchy menu → should show as Connected (not Unavailable).
  • Click an unavailable device → Retry → should connect and stay connected (not disconnect after a few seconds).

item.title: Beta 1.1.3 (build 49)
item.description:
Beta build 49 — stale-unavailable reconcile

For developer testing only. Public users will not see this update.

What changed

  • Out-of-band reconnect detection: If you connect a Magic device through System Settings → Bluetooth (bypassing Switchy), opening the Switchy menu now detects the connected device and moves it from "Unavailable Devices" to "Connected Devices" automatically.
  • Destructive click prevention: Clicking a device row that Switchy incorrectly shows as unavailable (but macOS already has connected) now silently reconciles instead of running the acquire flow — the unpair/re-pair recovery path is unreachable when the device is actually working.
  • Notification cleanup: "Device Unavailable" banners in Notification Center are dismissed automatically when reconciliation detects the device is locally connected.
  • Reentrancy guards: The acquire precheck and inverse menu pass re-validate operation locks after await suspension points to prevent race conditions between rapid user clicks and background reconciliation.
  • New NetworkBroadcasting / UserNotificationCenterRemoving test seams; 12 new test cases covering the reconcile path.

What to test

  • The reproduction case: Magic Trackpad connected to another Mac (no Switchy, lid closed) → connect it through System Settings on this Mac → open Switchy menu → device should appear under Connected Devices, not Unavailable.
  • Stale panel click: If you manage to click a stale Unavailable row before the menu refreshes, Retry should confirm the connection, NOT unpair the device.
  • No regression on normal switches: One-tap and batch switches with actual peer Macs should behave identically to build 48.
  • Notification Center: After a device becomes unavailable (peer goes offline) and then you reconnect it through System Settings, the "Device Unavailable" banner should disappear from Notification Center.

item.title: Beta 1.1.3 (build 48)
item.description:
Beta build 48 — usable HID connection verification

For developer testing only. Public users will not see this update.

What changed

  • Switchy no longer marks a Magic device as connected on Bluetooth-link success alone. A second HID-readiness gate runs after the existing connected probe before promoting the UI to connected.
  • Bluetooth-only success is now treated as provisional, not final — so the false-positive case (Trackpad shows connected in Switchy, but macOS Bluetooth menu disagrees and the pointer doesn't move) should be eliminated.
  • For orphaned devices last known to belong to another Mac, Switchy now prefers a clean fresh pair over reusing stale local pairing keys.
  • Connection-success decision centralized so single and batch flows can't drift apart.
  • New BluetoothManaging protocol seam; verifier is testable without real hardware (new InteractionLockTests coverage).
  • Logs now distinguish Bluetooth-link success from HID-ready success.

What to test

  • The original false-positive case: Magic Trackpad owned by MacBook A → power-cycle → switch to MacBook B → open MacBook A → power-cycle again → retry. Switchy on MacBook A should now NOT show success unless the Trackpad actually moves the pointer.
  • Normal one-tap switches: should feel essentially unchanged for already-usable devices.
  • Batch connect with 2+ devices: confirm no regression on the reliability you got from build 46/47.
  • Failed HID-ready connections: confirm the UI stays recoverable (no stuck false checkmark) and the retry path works.

item.title: Beta 1.1.3 (build 47)
item.description:
Beta build 47 — channels actually work now

For developer testing only. Public users will not see this update.

Why a manual install was required

Sparkle 2.x ignores the SUAllowedChannels Info.plist key unless an SPUUpdaterDelegate implements allowedChannels(for:). Builds 45 and 46 had the Info.plist key but no delegate, so Sparkle treated allowedChannels as an empty set and silently rejected every <sparkle:channel>beta</sparkle:channel> item — falling back to the newest untagged item (build 44) and showing "you're up to date".

What changed

  • Added SPUUpdaterDelegate conformance to SparkleUpdaterController with allowedChannels(for:) that reads SUAllowedChannels from Info.plist.
  • Stable Switchy: no SUAllowedChannels key → returns empty set → only sees untagged items (unchanged).
  • Switchy Beta: SUAllowedChannels=["beta"] → returns {"beta"} → sees beta-tagged items + untagged stable items.

What to test

  • After installing this build manually, future /switchy-build-beta runs should auto-update Switchy Beta within the Sparkle check interval (or via Check for Updates...).
  • The previously shipped behavior changes (post-connect settle gate from build 45, atomic batch acquire from build 46) are also in this build — so this is your active reliability test build.

item.title: Beta 1.1.3 (build 46)
item.description:
Beta build 46 — atomic batch acquire

For developer testing only. Public users will not see this update.

What changed

  • Replaced the two-phase batch acquire (pair-all → connect-all) with an atomic per-device acquire: each released device is paired AND connected to stable local ownership before the next one starts.
  • Field testing showed the previous shape could orphan D1 — freshly paired devices stayed disconnected through Phase 1, and the later Phase 2 connect occasionally collapsed because the controller was still settling. Reliability now wins over batch speed.
  • Recovery on a failed paired-device connect no longer escalates to a full re-acquire; it resets the in-progress flag and surfaces the failure cleanly.
  • Removed phase2StartSettle (no longer applicable). Inter-device settle is now a single value applied between atomic transactions.

What to test

  • Batch connect with 2+ devices: confirm both devices end up connected — D1 should no longer orphan in the failure modes you saw on previous betas.
  • Total time will be longer (we wait for D0 to fully settle before starting D1). Confirm it still feels acceptable.
  • Failure path: if the first device's atomic acquire fails, confirm Switchy moves on cleanly instead of stalling, and that the second device still gets a fair attempt.

item.title: Beta 1.1.3 (build 45)
item.description:
Beta build 45 — post-connect settle gate

For developer testing only. Public users will not see this update.

What changed

  • Added a "settle gate" after a fresh local connect: Switchy now waits for the device to remain locally connected for at least 1 continuous second (with two positive probes, timeout 5s) before promoting to success and starting the next batch/sequential operation.
  • Targets the rare second-device failure shape where Switchy moved on while D0's HID negotiation was still stabilizing.
  • Probe logic refactored into a single helper (probeLocalConnection); settle timing made injectable for tests via PostConnectSettleTiming.

What to test

  • Batch connect with 2+ devices: confirm no regression on speed and that the second device no longer occasionally fails to settle.
  • Sequential one-tap connects: should feel essentially unchanged (≤1s extra after each connect in the worst case).
  • Edge case: a device that connects then immediately drops — verify the gate resets correctly and we don't false-promote.

item.title: Version 1.1.3 (build 44)
item.description:
Build 44

  • Reframed the keep-awake setting around what it actually does for you: keeps this Mac available for switching while plugged in, including with the lid closed.
  • Settings now shows a live status — active on power, paused on battery, or degraded when closed-lid support isn't available — so you can tell at a glance whether this Mac is reachable.
  • When a device can't be reached because the host Mac is asleep, Switchy now points you to the relevant setting instead of leaving you guessing.

item.title: Version 1.1.3 (build 43)
item.description:
Build 43

  • Locks all device interactions while a switch is in progress, so a second device can't be tapped mid-operation.

item.title: Version 1.1.3 (build 42)
item.description:
Build 42

  • Rolled back the recent batch-connect changes while we keep working on the underlying issue.

item.title: Version 1.1.3 (build 41)
item.description:
Build 41

  • More reliable when switching a second device right after a first. Switchy now briefly pauses any already-connected tracked device that could interfere with pairing the new one, gives the new device a clean window to pair, then automatically reconnects the paused device.
  • "Connect all" batches now apply the same handoff between each device, so the second and third devices are as reliable as the first.
  • Smarter retry on the specific "no connection" pairing failure that occasionally hit follow-up switches — Switchy runs one targeted reset-and-retry instead of giving up.

item.title: Version 1.1.3 (build 40)
item.description:
Build 40

  • Tuned pair gate timings: fresh-pair window 20s, subsequent pair minimum gap since release 15s, subsequent pair spacing after success 7s.

item.title: Version 1.1.3 (build 39)
item.description:
Build 39

  • Pair gate between D0 and D1. Switchy now waits for D0 to reach a pair-ready state before attempting D1 in batch connect, preventing D1 failures caused by residual D0 connection state.

item.title: Version 1.1.3 (build 38)
item.description:
Build 38

  • Diagnostic instrumentation: detailed structured logging for fresh-pair operations (pre-pair state snapshot and post-failure diagnostics for both single and batch flows). No behavior changes.

item.title: Version 1.1.3 (build 37)
item.description:
What's New in 1.1.3 (build 37)

  • Sequential single-device switching: first device stays connected when the second one fails. Switchy now detects when another tracked device is already locally connected and suppresses the destructive Bluetooth power-cycle retry that was orphaning the surviving device.
  • Single-device release now uses the same auth-rejection window as batch release, so released devices are more likely to be pair-ready on the acquiring Mac.

Baseline hardening for the shared primary D1 failure. Isolates a D1 failure to just D1 and brings single-device release semantics into parity with batch.

item.title: Version 1.1.3 (build 36)
item.description:
Build 36

  • Batch Connect: if a device fails to pair on the first attempt, Switchy now retries once after 2.5 seconds before moving on. Addresses the intermittent "0x02 No Connection" error that occasionally hit the second device in multi-device batches.

Successful batches are unchanged. Failing pairs now take up to 2.5 seconds longer before surfacing the unavailable-device panel.

item.title: Version 1.1.3 (build 35)
item.description:
What's New in 1.1.3 (build 35)

  • Fixes the "D1 pair fails with 0x02" bug in batch connect: After each device is paired in Phase 1, the app now explicitly closes the leftover Bluetooth link before pairing the next device. This prevents the controller-level contention that was causing the second device to intermittently fail with "No Connection" errors.
  • Scales to 3-device batches (e.g. Mouse + Trackpad + Keyboard).

Root cause identified from build 34 diagnostic logs. See PRD for full details.

item.title: Version 1.1.3 (build 34)
item.description:
What's New in 1.1.3 (build 34)

  • Full diagnostic visibility for batch-connect failures: Bluetooth layer log messages (discovery poll outcome, pair attempt start, blueutil error strings) now show device details instead of "<private>". Closes the last diagnostic gap so any remaining D1 pair failures can be traced to their root cause.

No behavior changes. All logs are local to your Mac — nothing is sent anywhere.

item.title: Version 1.1.3 (build 33)
item.description:
What's New in 1.1.3 (build 33)

  • Improved batch-connect diagnostics: Log messages for "Connect all" operations now include device names, error messages, and timing details (previously hidden due to macOS privacy defaults). Helps diagnose any remaining intermittent batch-connect reliability issues.

No behavior changes. All logs are local to your Mac — nothing is sent anywhere.

item.title: Version 1.1.3 (build 32)
item.description:
What's New in 1.1.3 (build 32)

  • Clearer batch-connect error messages: When "Connect all" partially fails, the error dialog now shows the correct recovery action based on where the device actually is — no more misleading "Power off/on device" text when the peer Mac is still known.
  • Diagnostic logging for batch-connect timing: Added visibility into Phase 2 connect latency, HID profile readiness, and total batch wall-clock time. Helps diagnose any remaining intermittent reliability issues via Console.app with evidence rather than guesswork.

item.title: Version 1.1.3 (build 31)
item.description:
What's New in 1.1.3 (build 31)

  • 3-device batch reliability: Added a 1.5s settle between Phase 2 connects so HID profile negotiation on one device finishes before the next device's connection starts. Prevents false-negative connect failures when switching 3 devices (trackpad + mouse + keyboard) at once.

item.title: Version 1.1.3 (build 30)
item.description:
What's New in 1.1.3 (build 30)

  • Batch connect reliability (part 2): Fixed the cascading failure where if one device in a batch failed to pair, the already-connected device would also disconnect and orphan. A single device failure now stays isolated — your other devices keep working.
  • Improved first-try success for device 2+: Longer device-discovery window, wider inter-pair settle to avoid radio contention, and extended auth-rejection window for slower-firmware devices. Batch connect is ~2 seconds slower but noticeably more reliable.
  • Fewer false-negative connection errors: Post-connect verification now uses multi-attempt probes so the HID profile negotiation has time to complete before the app flags a connect as failed.

item.title: Version 1.1.3 (build 29)
item.description:
What's New in 1.1.3 (build 29)

  • Cold-start device status: Devices no longer show as "Unavailable" when reopening Switchy after being away — they now correctly appear as "Available" while syncing with your other Mac

item.title: Version 1.1.3 (build 28)
item.description:
What's New in 1.1.3 (build 28)

  • Batch connect reliability: Fixed intermittent failure where second device (mouse/keyboard) would fail to pair during batch connect — root cause was stale firmware keys causing slow advertising mode entry

item.title: Version 1.1.3 (build 27)
item.description:
What's New in 1.1.3 (build 27)

  • UI polish: Refresh button moved to section header, full-row tappable disclosure matching native macOS patterns

item.title: Version 1.1.3 (build 26)
item.description:
What's New in 1.1.3 (build 26)

  • UI polish: Simplified device scan layout with inline refresh and cleaner disclosure for non-Magic devices

item.title: Version 1.1.3 (build 25)
item.description:
What's New in 1.1.3 (build 25)

  • Smart device filtering: Device scan now shows Apple Magic devices first, with non-Magic devices tucked into a "Show all" disclosure
  • Refresh button: Re-scan paired devices in-place without re-triggering the full scan flow

item.title: Version 1.1.3 (build 24)
item.description:
What's New in 1.1.3 (build 24)

  • Batch connect reliability: Increased Phase 2 connect timeout for freshly paired devices — eliminates false timeouts during HID negotiation

item.title: Version 1.1.3 (build 23)
item.description:
What's New in 1.1.3 (build 23)

  • Fix: Wake race condition: "Connect all" no longer appears when a peer Mac just woke from sleep but its devices haven't reconnected yet — prevents failed switches that brick devices
  • Pre-validation: Switchy now verifies devices are actually available on the peer before starting a batch switch
  • Wake stabilization: After waking from sleep, Switchy automatically re-checks and broadcasts device states to peers in the background
  • Double-tap protection: Rapidly clicking "Connect all" no longer triggers duplicate operations

item.title: Version 1.1.3 (build 22)
item.description:
What's New in 1.1.3 (build 22)

  • Fix: Collateral device orphaning: When releasing one device to a peer, other connected devices on the same Mac are now automatically reconnected after Bluetooth powers back on — no more orphaned devices
  • Menu bar accuracy: Devices pending reconnection no longer flash as "Unavailable" during the brief Bluetooth power-off window
  • Concurrent release safety: Overlapping release requests no longer clobber each other's reconnection lists

item.title: Version 1.1.3 (build 21)
item.description:
What's New in 1.1.3 (build 21)

  • Two-phase batch connect: Pairs all devices first (no HID contention), then connects — eliminates 0x02 pairing failures
  • Pipelined batch release: Unpairs all devices at once instead of one-by-one — faster release
  • Pre-flight reliability: Unpair verification + power cycle ordering fix prevents stale pairing records
  • Menu bar detection: Fixed device availability showing incorrectly after stale announcements
  • Instant visual feedback: Menu bar icon starts pulsing immediately on Connect All

item.title: Version 1.1.3 (build 20)
item.description:
What's New in 1.1.3 (build 20)

  • Batch connection reliability: Improved batch connection flow for more reliable multi-device switching
  • Settings cleanup: Removed social links from About section, updated copyright

item.title: Version 1.1.3 (build 19)
item.description:
What's New in 1.1.3 (build 19)

  • Progress indicator: Menu bar icon now shows a circular progress ring during device connection instead of a pulsing animation, with a checkmark on completion
  • Device availability fix: Fixed devices incorrectly showing as unavailable when connected on peer Mac due to stale announcement handling

item.title: Version 1.1.3 (build 18)
item.description:
What's New in 1.1.3 (build 18)

  • Auto update check fix: Automatic update checks now actually run on launch when enabled, instead of requiring manual check

item.title: Version 1.1.3 (build 17)
item.description:
What's New in 1.1.3 (build 17)

  • Faster batch switching: Pre-flight power cycle clears stale controller state before acquiring, eliminating per-device retry delays
  • Reduced failed connections: Stale pairing keys are cleared upfront so devices don't waste time on doomed connect-first attempts
  • Batch reliability: Single-attempt acquire per device prevents power cycles that would kill previously connected devices

item.title: Version 1.1.3 (build 16)
item.description:
What's New in 1.1.3 (build 16)

  • Faster switching: Speed optimizations across connection, retry, and Bluetooth pairing flows
  • Parallel Bluetooth connect: Orphaned devices now reconnect in parallel for significantly faster batch connections
  • Stale key cleanup: Clears outdated Bluetooth keys to prevent ghost connection failures
  • Smarter retries: Faster retry logic with improved connection time for saves
  • Production hardening: Code review fixes for improved stability and reliability
  • Zscaler compatibility: Uses active connections when Zscaler is present

item.title: Beta 1.1.4 (build 53)
item.description:
Beta build 53 — second-device orphan fix

Tester focus: batch / multi-device handoff. Connect-all from one Mac to another, especially the second device in a batch — that was the one that intermittently orphaned (0x02/719). Watch for any device that fails to pair, gets actively rejected, or re-attaches to the releasing Mac mid-handoff.

  • Root cause fixed: the releasing Mac's power-on and the acquiring Mac's pair-gate were both anchored to the same 15s instant, so the second fresh pair began exactly as the releasing radio returned. Now event-driven instead of time-tuned.
  • New pairPhaseComplete signal: the acquiring Mac tells the releasing Mac when its pair-critical window ends (on success and final failure), routed through the existing early power-on accounting.
  • Single-release fallback demoted to a 45s safety net with a unit-tested invariant that it exceeds the acquiring side's worst-case fresh-pair window.
  • releasesInProgress guard: collateral reconnection can no longer --connect a device a release handler currently owns.
  • runBlueutil: a queued command that timed out or was cancelled can no longer execute later (no zombie execution); queue-wait excluded from the timeout budget, separate 30s queue-wait ceiling.

Known risk: timing changes touch the batch handoff path directly — exercise repeated back-to-back batch switches.

item.title: Version 1.1.2 (build 15)
item.description:
What's New in 1.1.2 (build 15)

  • Stability release: Rebuilt from a clean, stable baseline for improved reliability
  • Removed Wake-on-LAN: WOL removed to eliminate connection issues; wake your other Mac manually before switching
  • Connect-all reliability: Fixed retry failures, first-attempt issues, and flaky batch connections
  • Cleaner settings: Removed social links from the About pane

item.title: Version 1.1.2 (build 14)
item.description:
What's New in 1.1.2 (build 14)

  • Connect Here Now: New option to pair a device directly on this Mac when the other Mac is offline or sleeping
  • Manual release: Release a connected device from the menu bar to hand it off to another Mac
  • Broader acquire recovery: Falls back to all discovered peers when the expected peer is unreachable
  • Faster wake recovery: Preserves peer history across sleep for quicker reconnection
  • Smarter retry logic: Only forces peer refresh when discovery isn't already active

item.title: Version 1.1.2 (build 13)
item.description:
What's New in 1.1.2 (build 13)

  • Auto-recovery: Switchy now silently retries once when a peer is reachable but didn't respond, reducing unnecessary error panels
  • Smarter error panels: Three distinct messages depending on what's wrong — Mac asleep, Mac still waking up, or device not connected
  • Targeted acquire: Requests now target the expected peer directly instead of broadcasting to all peers
  • Better diagnostics: Correlation IDs in logs for easier troubleshooting

item.title: Version 1.1.2 (build 12)
item.description:
What's New in 1.1.2

  • Removed Wake-on-LAN: WOL support removed for reliability; wake and unlock the other Mac manually before connecting
  • Cleaned up About pane: Removed social links for a cleaner settings UI
  • Connect-all reliability: Fixed retry failures and first-attempt issues when connecting all devices

item.title: Version 1.1.1 (build 11)
item.description:
What's New in 1.1.1 (build 11)

WOL & Connect-All Reliability

  • Hardened Wake-on-LAN: More reliable peer waking with improved diagnostics
  • Fixed connect-all flow: Resolved retry failures and first-attempt issues when connecting all devices
  • Better error recovery: Connect-all now ensures initial connection succeeds before proceeding

item.title: Version 1.1.1 (build 10)
item.description:
What's New in 1.1.1 (build 10)

Bluetooth Reliability

  • Retry logic for release operations: Unpair, disconnect, and power-off now retry once on failure for more reliable device handoffs
  • Connection verification: Dual-probe verification (with retries) confirms devices are actually connected or disconnected after operations
  • Separated single/batch power-on tracking: Single-device and batch flows no longer cancel each other's Bluetooth power-on timers
  • Adaptive batch timeouts: Fallback delays now scale with number of released devices for better multi-device reliability
  • Stale task protection: Token-based guards prevent outdated fallback tasks from firing unexpectedly

item.title: Version 1.1.1 (build 9)
item.description:
What's New in 1.1.1 (build 9)

Smarter Network Awareness

  • Subnet-aware Wake-on-LAN: WOL only offered when peer is on the same network
  • New "Sleeping Devices" section: Clear separation of wakeable devices in menu
  • New "Out of Network" section: Guidance when Macs are on different networks
  • Better batch connect: Zero-release protection and partial success handling
  • Reduced false negatives: Enhanced Bluetooth ownership checks during state transitions

item.title: Version 1.1.1 (build 8)
item.description:
What's New in 1.1.1 (build 8)

Faster Error Feedback

  • Reduced error wait times: Worst-case error display reduced from 57s to 25-35s through parallel operations
  • Parallel WOL + acquire: Wake-on-LAN now runs simultaneously with device requests (saves 10-15s)
  • Smarter zombie detection: Probes discovered peers as hints for earlier zombie recovery
  • Better status feedback: More granular progress messages during device switching
  • Background health monitoring: Advisory-only peer health checks for improved reliability

item.title: Version 1.1.1 (build 7)
item.description:
What's New in 1.1.1 (build 7)

Network Robustness

  • Improved discovery timing: Increased wait times for more reliable device detection
  • Fixed Bonjour race condition: Resolved browser initialization timing issue
  • Better WOL offline detection: More accurate sleep/offline state identification

item.title: Version 1.1.1 (build 6)
item.description:
What's New in 1.1.1 (build 6)

Reliability Improvements

  • Zombie peer detection: Better handling of stale device connections
  • Partial sleep fixes: Improved detection when devices enter low-power states
  • Faster failure detection: Reduced timing for identifying unresponsive peers

item.title: Version 1.1.1 (build 5)
item.description:
What's New in 1.1.1 (build 5)

Wake-on-LAN Improvements

  • Faster wake times: Proactive WOL fires immediately when menu opens
  • Accurate errors: Shows real error cause instead of generic "Couldn't wake"
  • Smarter detection: No false "Sleeping" labels during discovery
  • No WOL spam: 60-second cooldown prevents duplicate wake attempts
  • Smoother UI: No more flicker when rapidly opening/closing menu

item.title: Version 1.1.1
item.description:
What's New in 1.1.1

  • Improved device detection

item.title: Version 1.1.0
item.description:
What's New in 1.1.0

  • Notification improvements
  • Improved device discovery during menu bar icon open
  • Automatic update checks every 4 hours

item.title: Version 1.0.0
item.description:
What's New

  • Initial release of Switchy
  • Quick Bluetooth device switching from menu bar
  • Automatic device connection management
  • Battery level monitoring
  • Clean, native macOS interface
